steps.title: Use the equivalence to set up an equation in p
steps.content: Two polynomial expressions are equivalent when their corresponding coefficients are equal. The original expression contains the constant $p$, and it must be equivalent to $6x^2 - 155x + 24$. So expand the original expression (distributing any binomial factors) and collect terms in $x^2$, $x$, and the constant.

steps.title: Match the coefficient of x to solve for p
steps.content: After expanding, equate the coefficient of $x$ in the original expression with $-155$ (the coefficient of $x$ in the target). This produces a linear equation in $p$. Solving that linear equation gives $p = 7$.

steps.title: Confirm with the other coefficients
steps.content: Substitute $p = 7$ back into the original expression and verify that the $x^2$ coefficient is $6$ and the constant term is $24$, matching $6x^2 - 155x + 24$ on every term. That matches choice B — B is correct.
